title: Yankees Rivalry Roundup: Red Sox make it a dozen wins in a row
description: Boston is back over .500 for the first time since Opening Day.
fullText:
The Yankees were rained out on Saturday, meaning they face a pivotal day-night doubleheader on Sunday against the Dodgers with a chance to win the series in one go. Severe storms in the eastern half of the country have led to a handful of rainouts and doubleheaders to open the second half, all of which had implications on divisional races around the AL. The Red Sox swept their two games against the Rays on Friday while the Pirates had an opportunity to do the same to the Guardians on Saturday to ratchet up the drama in the East and Central.

The Red Sox rode a seventh-inning comeback to take the first three games of this four game set from the first-place Rays, giving Boston a 12-game winning streak. Neither of the starting pitchers pulled their weight, Ian Seymour lasting just three innings for Tampa after allowing three runs on two hits and two walks while Patrick Sandoval allowed five runs (four earned) on nine hits and a walk in five innings.

The Rays opened the scoring in the second as Ryan Vilade led off with a double and scored on a Chandler Simpson single, the speedy left fielder then advancing to second on a throwing error and scoring on a Nick Fortes RBI single. Boston responded with a pair in the bottom-half, Andruw Monasterio drawing an ABS-assisted walk and scoring on a two-run homer from Jahmai Jones. They grabbed a lead the following inning on a solo shot from Wilyer Abreu, but Tampa answered with three in the fourth on a Simpson leadoff double, a Victor Mesa Jr. two-run blast, Richie Palacios double, and Yandy Díaz RBI single, though Díaz would be thrown out at the plate to end the inning on an ensuing double by Jonny DeLuca.

That out at home would prove the difference, as despite Tampa adding an insurance run on a solo shot from DeLuca in the seventh, Boston roared back with four runs in the bottom of that frame to grab the decisive lead. Monasterio led off with a double and advanced to third as Jarren Duran reached on a throwing error by Vilade to put runners on the corners with no outs. Masataka Yoshida brought Monasterio home with an RBI groundout and Ceddanne Rafaela plated Duran with an RBI double to draw within one. This brought Abreu to the plate to demolish his second home run of the game, this one a two-run shot to complete the comeback.

With the win, the Red Sox move above .500 for the first time since Opening Day. In the span of two weeks, they have gone from 15.5 games back in last place to third place and only seven games back of first.

Pittsburgh Pirates (51-47) 7, Cleveland Guardians (51-47) 1 (Game 1)

The rainout on Friday set up a Saturday day-night doubleheader between these two Rust Belt rivals. Jared Jones pitched six perfect innings last time out against the Braves and he followed that up with another dominant display in the first game of the doubleheader, tossing five innings of one-run ball on three hits and a walk against nine strikeouts. His offense did their scoring in bunches, plating two in the second on a two-run bomb by Jacob Gonzalez, three in the sixth on a two-run tank from Esmerlyn Valdez and a solo shot by Nick Gonzales, and two in the eighth on a wild pitch and a Ryan O’Hearn sac fly.

Cleveland Guardians (52-47) 5, Pittsburgh Pirates (51-48) 3 (Game 2)

Guardians rookie Travis Bazzana had himself quite the day. After tripling in his team’s only run in the early game, Bazzana won the nightcap with his first career walk-off home run. Pittsburgh scored the first three runs of the contest, one in each of the second, third, and fourth on a Brandon Lowe sac fly, Esmerlyn Valdez RBI double, and Jake Mangum RBI single. That lead held until the eighth when the Pirates bullpen imploded. Cleveland scored three in the eighth on singles by Steven Kwan, Brayan Rocchio, Chase DeLauter, and Kahlil Watson, aided by a pair of wild pitches. That set up the defining moment for Bazzana in the ninth. Patrick Bailey led off with a single but was thrown out at home on a Kwan double. However, Bazzana picked up his teammate with a two-run walk-off home run to draw the Guardians level with the White Sox atop the AL Central, Chicago remaining nominally ahead on winning percentage.

Toronto Blue Jays (46-52) 1, Chicago White Sox (51-46) 0

The Blue Jays suddenly find themselves in last place, so perhaps it is fitting that Shane Bieber pitched his best start of the year — six shutout innings allowing three hits and two walks against six strikeouts — just in time to be shopped at the Trade Deadline. The three relievers that followed kept the shutout intact, allowing just a hit and a walk in the final three frames. The lone run of the contest came in the fourth, Vladimir Guerrero Jr. reaching on a one-out double and scoring on a George Springer RBI single.

Texas Rangers (50-48) 7, Atlanta Braves (56-41) 6

The Rangers became the first team in the AL West to reach 50 wins with a nail-biter against the Braves. After Joc Pederson led off the game with a homer Atlanta jumped out to an early 4-1 lead, Eli White hitting a two-run homer, Drake Baldwin an RBI single, and Ozzie Albies a sac fly in the second. Texas cut that deficit to one courtesy of RBI singles by Wyatt Langford and Brandon Nimmo in the third, but a Michael Harris II leadoff homer in the bottom of the frame restored Atlanta’s two-run cushion. In the sixth, it was the Rangers’ turn to score four runs, the first pair on an Evan Carter two-run blast to tie the game, 5-5, and the second pair on a two-run single from Nimmo with the bases loaded. Mauricio Dubón cut the deficit to one with an RBI double seventh, but Texas would hold on from there for the 7-6 win.

Seattle Mariners (49-50) 4, San Francisco Giants (42-56) 3

The Guardians weren’t the only team to win it with the last swing of the game, the Mariners avenging last night’s shutout loss with an extra-innings walk-off win over the Giants. In a battle of staff aces having relatively down years neither pitcher came out on top, Logan Webb allowing three runs on two hits and a walk in 6.2 innings while Bryan Woo allowed three runs (two earned) on three hits and two walks in six innings. San Francisco scored the first three runs of the game, rookie Bryce Eldridge lining an RBI single in the third and Rafael Devers and Willy Adames crushing a pair of solo home runs in the sixth. Cole Young responded with a three-run homer in the seventh to send the game to extras. In the bottom of the tenth, a Colt Emerson sac bunt advanced the automatic runner to third, and after a J.P. Crawford walk, Julio Rodríguez hit a ball just deep enough to left to bring Victor Robles home as the game-winning run.

title: Spain or Argentina, who will win the FIFA World Cup? Ex-player predictions
description: Former player predictions as the European and South American champions clash in a World Cup final for the first time.
fullText:
The FIFA World Cup 2026 final is not far away, and the predictions are coming in thick and fast.

Spain, the reigning European champions, are eyeing their second World Cup title, 16 years on from their maiden triumph in South Africa. But standing in their way are holders Argentina, the South American giants aiming to become just the third country to win back-to-back World Cup titles.

Stats provider Opta predicts Spain as the favourites to win the 2026 World Cup, with a 59.56 percent probability of lifting the trophy in New Jersey. La Roja are also the picks of several former players and pundits.

Former England striker Michael Owen said world number two Spain are the “deserved” winners of the tournament.

“Spain haven’t got the greatest individual players, I think France probably have … but Spain as a team are everything England are not – they are brave, courageous with the ball,” Owen told The Straits Times.

“Spain are just a great team that pass the ball confidently and play amazing football as a collective.”

Zlatan Ibrahimovic, the ex-Barcelona and Manchester United striker who represented Sweden at two World Cups, expects Spain to dominate the final, which comes two years on from their Euro 2024 triumph.

Spain handled France very good [in the semifinals]. I believe they can handle this Argentina,” Zlatan, now a pundit on US broadcaster Fox Sports, said.

“Argentina is not a counterattacking team. Spain are going to do their thing. They’re going to play collectively … I believe Spain are going to dominate that game.”

Three lions fell just short but nothing but pride for this country…

Going 2-1 Spain tomorrow! 
Lamine Yamal ⚽️⚽️ https://t.co/CBw7Gxykiw

— Rio Ferdinand (@rioferdy5) July 18, 2026

Spain were also John Terry’s favourites for glory, with the former England captain saying on the Sports Uncensored show that Luis de la Fuente’s side have “got too much for Argentina”.

Former goalkeeper Iker Casillas, the Spain captain during their maiden World Cup triumph in 2010, expressed his confidence in the team’s ability to bring home a second, sharing a picture of another star halfway stitched on the top of their badge.

A star represents every World Cup a team has won.

Y por qué no? pic.twitter.com/IB9wTlcGYD

— Iker Casillas (@IkerCasillas) July 16, 2026

While a lot of the consensus centred around a Spain win, some think Argentina have a chance, even if it is slim.

Alan Shearer, one of England’s greatest strikers of all time, backed the reigning champions, saying on The Rest is Football podcast, “If there’s one team that can do it in terms of stopping them [Spain], it’s Argentina because of their mentality and togetherness.

“They have been down so many times and in trouble, and somehow managed to get a win. That’s what you’ve got to admire about them.

“When you’ve got a genius in your side,” he added, referring to Messi, “you’ve always got a chance.”

But how can Argentina beat favourites Spain in the title clash?

English football pundit Micah Richards thinks the answer to that is low-block transition – the strategy of teams sitting back closest to their goal and, after winning possession, rapidly shifting from a compact defensive shape into an offensive counterattack

“Get the ball to Messi, whereas [Julian] Alvarez or [Lisandro] Martinez just make it horrible, make it how they did with England,” he added.

“I think Argentina need to stay in the game as they did against England and turn it on when they see a shift in momentum.”

Meanwhile, another of England’s greats, Gary Lineker, stuck with his pretournament pick, Spain, but added that Argentina are “so gritty”, making the predictions a very tough choice.

“You cannot go against that little fella [Messi]. He’s so great; it’s going to be really, really close. It’s genuinely 50-50.”

Unlike others, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani refused to pick a side.

“Everyone I root for has lost,” he said, with a laugh. “So I feel like whoever I say is going to lose at this point.

“I’m gonna say the classic politician’s answer – I’m excited for a good game.”

title: Oregon Ducks ranked No. 2 in preseason composite rankings
description: With goals of winning the program's first national title in 2026, where do the Oregon Ducks land in preseason composite rankings?
fullText:
Although rankings in college football only matter once the College Football Playoff rankings are released in November and December, it's always intriguing to see what the national media's view on the Oregon Ducks is.

From the outside looking in, they're a program that has been excellent during the regular season but hasn't been able to get it done in the biggest of games, falling in the CFP Quarterfinal and CFP Semifinal in consecutive years. But from within the building, the task at hand is attacking the regular season and putting themselves in the best position for a deep playoff run.

ESPN's FPI projections give the Ducks a 9.6% chance of winning the national title, which is their main goal entering the 2026 season. FPI also provided rankings, slotting Oregon at No. 4 behind Ohio State (No. 1), Texas (No. 2) and Notre Dame (No. 3). But that's just a projection system, and not quite a grasp of what the media's opinion is of the Ducks heading into the fall.

To fully grasp where the Ducks stand in the rankings, SuperWest Sports provided a preseason composite top 25 rankings from 23 different media outlets such as USA TODAY, CBS Sports and ESPN. While FPI has Oregon at No. 4, SuperWest Sports' composite rankings have the Ducks tabbed as the No. 2 team in the nation.

They're the top-ranked team in the west, with USC (No. 12), BYU (No. 13), Washington (No. 19), Utah (No. 20), Arizona (No. 24) and Boise State and Cal (tied for No. 25) falling behind them.

Crucial, highly anticipated games against USC and Washington are on Oregon's 2026 schedule, with both potentially carrying heavy CFP implications. Dominating the west will go a long way towards the Ducks reaching their goals this coming season.

Contact/Follow @Ducks_Wire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Oregon Ducks news, notes, and opinions.

This article originally appeared on Ducks Wire: Oregon Ducks ranked No. 2 in preseason composite rankings

title: Kobie Turner poised to reach new heights with Myles Garrett aboard
description: Kobie Turner has already been highly productive for the Rams, but with Myles Garrett now in the mix, he's bound to get even better.
fullText:
Entering the 2026 NFL season, the Los Angeles Rams have put together the most impressive pass-rushing unit in the NFL – at least on paper. While only time will tell how the defenders on the line will mesh, the prospect of seeing Myles Garrett and Kobie Turner attacking quarterbacks will be must-watch TV. Especially with an offense that will force the opposition to pass.

Both players were recently honored by Pro Football Network, being named to the site's list of the 100 best players in the NFL. Garrett, the record-holder for single-season sacks and the current Defensive Player of the Year, was unsurprisingly ranked third, only behind quarterbacks Josh Allen and Lamar Jackson. Current NFL MVP Matthew Stafford was ranked 14th.

Turner is a player who divides opinion mainly due to a lack of a narrative being written about him, often causing his outstanding play to be overlooked. He was properly honored by PFN with a ranking of No. 66. Turner has come a long way since being a late bloomer in college.

"It’s hard to imagine that Kobie Turner wasn’t even invited to the NFL Combine heading into the league in 2023," wrote PFN's Jacob Infante. "Not only was he an impact player from the moment the Rams drafted him, but he’s been a steady force along their interior defensive line who hasn’t missed a single regular season game in his three years with the team.

"With 24.0 sacks since 2023, Turner’s total is the second-highest among all defensive tackles since he entered the league. He’s ranked in the top ten in PFN DT Impact Scoring each of his last two seasons, most recently finishing seventh in 2025. He’s in the last year of his rookie deal and feels primed for a massive contract in the open market."

Now, Turner is pairing his high sack rate with the NFL sack king. Considering the improvements made to the Rams' secondary and their other teammates on the defensive line, Garrett and Turner are in a prime position to become the NFL's next top wrecking crew.

This article originally appeared on Rams Wire: Kobie Turner poised to reach new heights with Myles Garrett aboard

title: RB Leipzig set to win race for Dortmund- and Barcelona-linked Hoffenheim star
description: TSG Hoffenheim striker Fisnik Asllani is reportedly on the verge of a move to RB Leipzig.According to Bild, the 23-year-old is expected to complete his switch to Die Roten Bullen before the end of the...
fullText:
TSG Hoffenheim striker Fisnik Asllani is reportedly on the verge of a move to RB Leipzig.

According to Bild, the 23-year-old is expected to complete his switch to Die Roten Bullen before the end of the month.

Leipzig are planning to trigger the €30 million release clause in Asllani's contract, which runs until 2029.

Barcelona and Borussia Dortmund have also been mentioned as suitors for the forward.

However, the Catalan giants reportedly only saw Asllani as a backup to their primary target, Atlético Madrid striker Julián Álvarez, while Dortmund would need to free up space in both their squad and wage budget before pursuing a move for the striker.

As a result, Leipzig appear set to win the race for one of the Bundesliga's most highly-rated young attackers.

title: The Daily: Unlucky 7’s; Second Time Seems to be the Charm
description: The post The Daily: Unlucky 7’s; Second Time Seems to be the Charm appeared first on Detroit Hockey Now. Maybe you don’t know this, but the owner of the Red Wings is also the owner of the Detroit Tigers. […] The post The Daily: Unlucky 7’s; Second Time Seems to be the Charm appeared first on Detroit Hockey Now.
fullText:
The post The Daily: Unlucky 7’s; Second Time Seems to be the Charm appeared first on Detroit Hockey Now.

Maybe you don’t know this, but the owner of the Red Wings is also the owner of the Detroit Tigers. Chris Ilitch has managed both the Tigers and Red Wings since the passing of his father, the beloved “Mr.I.”

While the younger Ilitch still hasn’t found quite the same success as his forebear when it comes to either sporting venture, fans of both may have been able to see some parallels in the organizations, especially these last few years as behind the scenes more and more operations begin to fall under a general “Ilitch umbrella.”

One thing you may not realize, however, is that with Steve Yzerman stepping aside as general manager of the Red Wings, Ilitch has now started to establish a pattern with his roster managers.

Both Yzerman and former Tigers GM Al Avila were given seven years to turn their respective franchises around.

Both were “legacy” managers known from their time spent working closely with the elder Ilitch.

And both also ultimately failed to achieve the goal of bringing their team out of a rebuild phase.

However, in that parallel there should also be a glimmer for hope. Some of Al Avila’s draft picks (not all, but a couple, mind you) are major contributors to what is now a contending team. Led by an exciting rookie season from breakout star Kevin McGonigle, the Tigers are looking to get back into the playoffs this year even with their struggles on the mound.

The key for the Tigers was turning the keys over to a young, outside mind in Scott Harris who modernized the Tigers front office and as President of Baseball Operations, got them back into the playoffs after a decade long drought.

The Red Wings will have three of their prospects in Detroit later this week at the Belfor Training Center in preparation for the World Junior Summer Showcase for the IIHF. JP Hurlbert, Victor Plante, and Brent Solomon will be in attendance for Team USA.

Carter Bear did receive an invite for Team Canada but declined due to a conflicting schedule. Notably, the Red Wings do not have any Swedish prospects going to the junior showcase.

The Red Wings most recent first round pick has spent his summer in Michigan, though it hasn’t all been related to the team who drafted him Downtown.

Jakub Kindl is considering hanging up the skates after winning a championship at the ripe age of 39.

Pittsburgh Hockey Now: Dan Kingerski believes that a new GM in Detroit means a faster rebuild for Pittsburgh.

Colorado Hockey Now: Chris MacFarland continues to add former Avs to the Nashville Predators.

Florida Hockey Now: George Richards theorizes on who is going to lead the Panthers in scoring this year.

The post The Daily: Unlucky 7’s; Second Time Seems to be the Charm appeared first on Detroit Hockey Now.

title: What Scottie Scheffler told Ted Scott about his ball-striking which left him shocked when they started working together
description: Ted Scott revealed that Scottie Scheffler claimed that his ball-striking was a weakness when they first began to work together, as he reflected on being on the bag for the world number one. It has been a truly remarkable few years for Scheffler.
fullText:
Ted Scott revealed that Scottie Scheffler claimed that his ball-striking was a weakness when they first began to work together, as he reflected on being on the bag for the world number one.

It has been a truly remarkable few years for Scheffler. The 30-year-old went into the 2022 season on the PGA Tour still awaiting a first victory.

As the 2026 major championship season draws to a close, Scheffler can now reflect on four major victories, with 16 further wins coming on the PGA Tour.

Ted Scott has been his caddie for the large majority of that time. The pair began working together shortly after Scheffler made his Ryder Cup debut back in 2021.

Incredibly, it seems that one person who did not see such success just around the corner was Scottie Scheffler himself.

Speaking in a past Bible Caddie podcast, Scott explained what Scheffler identified as a problem within his game when their partnership began.

“It’s really amazing especially because when I first started working for him, he told me, he said, ‘I’m not a very good ball-striker’. And after week one, I was like, ‘buddy, are you sure you’re not a very good ball-striker? Who’s telling you this? What’s wrong with you? You hit everything directly at the tree I’m pointing at, driver included’,” he said.

“It’s interesting how we perceive ourselves. He’s a very confident man, he has a belief in himself, and he’s a really hard worker, super hard worker. Humble, he’ll ask for help, he’s not afraid to tell his team, ‘look, I’m struggling with this, can you help me figure this out?’ He’s just got a really cool heart to be great, and it’s been amazing to stand [next to him].”

In fairness to Scheffler, he had good reason to think that his ball-striking could be better.

He was actually 83rd on tour for approach play during the 2021 season. It is easy to forget now that he was a slightly contentious pick for the Ryder Cup at Whistling Straits.

The 2026 season is likely to be his worst season for strokes gained approach since then.

However, he was fourth in 2022, before finishing first in that category for three seasons in a row. So it is no wonder that Scott was left completely baffled by Scheffler’s criticism of himself.

Scheffler’s challenge for 2027 will be to return to that sort of level after a year which does look set to be a disappointment by his incredible standards.

title: Clairey Lin claims 2026 U.S. Girls’ Junior after dominant week at Old Chatham
description: British Columbia teen Clairey Lin dominated the field to win the 2026 U.S. Girls' Junior title in North Carolina.
fullText:
For the second straight year, a Canadian has won one of the biggest junior tournaments in the world.

Clairey Lin, a 16-year-old from British Columbia, dominated all week at Old Chatham in Durham, North Carolina, claiming the 2026 U.S. Girls' Junior title in controlling fashion. Lin took home medalist honors on Tuesday and then won six matches, including against USNDP member Amelie Zalsman in the championship match, walking away with a 5-and-4 victory. Lin becomes the first player since Rose Zhang in 2021 to claim medalist honors and go on to win the U.S. Girls' Junior.

“I’m still processing, but yeah, I've dreamed of it my entire life,” Lin said. “I've always wanted to be on the big stage and inspire some people and work hard and see it pay off, and to be able to do that today is amazing."

Aphrodite Deng became the first Canadian champion of the U.S. Girls' Junior last year. Lin's dominance helped keep the trophy north of the border.

Lin birdied the first hole of the 36-hole championship match Saturday to go 1 up, but the two continued trading blows with Zalsman eventually going 1 up thru 6. Then Lin went on another one of her runs, making four straight birdies and taking complete control. Throughout the course of the week – two stroke-play rounds and six in match play, – Lin made at least one birdie on every hole at Old Chatham, a true indicator of how in control she was.

“It was a tiring (match) the entire way," Lin said. "I think it (came down to) just persevering more than anything, not let anything get to me, not let the bad shots take too big a toll on anything mental, I kept moving and knew that it's a 36-hole match and anything can happen."

With the win, Lin gets an exemption into the 2027 U.S. Women's Open at Inverness in Ohio. She also has a spot in next month's U.S. Women's Amateur at the Honors Course in Ooltewah, Tennessee.

Cameron Jourdan is an assistant editor of Golfweek, covering college and amateur golf, the PGA Tour and plenty more. Follow Cameron on X/Twitter (@Cam_Jourdan) or Instagram (@GolfweekJourdan).

This article originally appeared on Golfweek: Clairey Lin claims 2026 U.S. Girls’ Junior after dominant week at Old Chatham

title: Saka says painful World Cup exit will fuel England for next challenge
description: Bukayo Saka says the “pain” of their World Cup semi-final defeat will fuel England as they attempt to finally end the nation’s long wait for a trophy. The Arsenal winger scored a stunning hat-trick in Saturday night’s remarkable 6-4 bronze-medal match victory over France in Miami which ensured he and his team-mates will return home with something tangible to show for their hard work.
fullText:
Bukayo Saka says the “pain” of their World Cup semi-final defeat will fuel England as they attempt to finally end the nation’s long wait for a trophy.

The Arsenal winger scored a stunning hat-trick in Saturday night’s remarkable 6-4 bronze-medal match victory over France in Miami which ensured he and his team-mates will return home with something tangible to show for their hard work.

However, the 24-year-old, who missed a penalty in the shootout defeat by Italy in the Euro 2020 final, will do so admitting the memories of Wednesday night’s semi-final exit at the hands of Argentina will simply increase his determination to succeed.

Saka told reporters: “Looking back at it when we go to the next major tournament, this will definitely be fuel for us. It’s very tough to take, but we did show and we believe – and I feel that we made the country believe as well – that we could do it.

“That belief hasn’t left us. We’ll be back next tournament to try to get over the line.”

The fact that England managed to regroup and get the better of Didier Deschamps’ men in a madcap game in Miami four days after their hopes of winning the World Cup were dashed by Lionel Messi and company is worthy of praise after a bruising post-mortem.

Saka said: “I don’t think anything can lessen the pain that we felt from the other night. That one really hurt us, I’ll be honest.

“But today was just about being professional and finishing strongly. This group has got that togetherness, we’ve got that mentality – that’s never been a problem for us – and we showed that today, so we can be proud of ourselves to achieve that highest finish that England has seen (since 1966).”

Saka became just the fourth Englishman to score a hat-trick at the World Cup finals when he converted an 87th-minute spot-kick to hold off a French fightback, and that too was a source of pride.

He said: “As a collective, this is what we discussed. We deserve to finish strongly for the tournament that we’ve had, we deserve to finish in the highest position that England has seen for 60 years.

“This group deserves nothing less and that was the mentality, and you saw that in the first half. We played a perfect first half and ultimately that won us the game.

“On a personal level as well, I’ve worked so hard to try to get myself ready for this tournament and be available to produce moments like that, and for me, it’s an incredible achievement.

“There are only three other Englishmen to have done it, so it’s a nice record to have now and I’m proud of myself to achieve that.”

Tottenham defender Djed Spence, who revealed he and his team-mates had shut out the “outside noise” in the wake of their semi-final defeat, backed head coach Thomas Tuchel to get it right.

He said: “He’s a great manager and we’ve built something special. It wasn’t what we wanted, but it’s stepping stones and it’s the best we’ve finished since 1966, so it’s a step in the right direction.

“Hopefully in the next tournament we can bring something home.”

title: Dan Lanning sets the standard Lincoln Riley needs to match at USC
description: Dan Lanning has positioned the Oregon Ducks where they need to be in the college football world. Playoff wins are what Lincoln Riley lacks
fullText:
The Oregon Ducks, since coming into the Big Ten, have raised their standard of play in the conference. Dan Lanning has put the Ducks in the College Football Playoff the past two seasons. With the continuity and talent the Ducks have on both sides of the ball, this should be their year. Lincoln Riley and USC want it to be the Trojans' year instead.

In a recent article, Zachary Neel breaks down how Dan Lanning is viewed among top coaches in the country:

"According to Wasserman, Lanning is the No. 3 head coach in the conference, behind defending national champion Curt Cignetti and Indiana, and former defending national champion Ryan Day at Ohio State.

"After being blown out in the College Football Playoff two consecutive years, Oregon and Lanning are the brunt of many jokes," Wasserman writes. "People view the Ducks as a very good team, but not one that will ever be good enough to break through in the CFP. That’s wrong. And dangerous. Though Oregon has not been the best team in the country at any point during Lanning’s leadership, it has steadily been a force in talent acquisition and retention. Lanning is 48-8 overall, and his teams will consistently make the CFP. Oregon may not have won a national title under his leadership, but it seems only a matter of time before it breaks through. Heck, it could be in 2026."

Dan Lanning has consistently kept his teams in the conversation. Unfortunately, he hasn't been able to lead his teams past his counterparts. Ryan Day and Curt Cignetti have the consistency and the championship. That is what Dan Lanning is hoping to achieve this season. Lincoln Riley is currently just chasing what Dan Lanning has been able to cultivate in the Big Ten. Riley, this season, for sure needs to put USC in the conversation.

This article originally appeared on Trojans Wire: Dan Lanning sets the standard Lincoln Riley needs to match at USC
